text:
In a quiet village nestled among rolling hills, the annual Harvest Festival was a delight for all its residents. Each house had hung banners celebrating crops like cornfields, apples trees, and vibrant pumpkins. Children dashed around, collecting ribbons for their mom's pies, while adults toasted with sparkling cider.

There was a young girl named Lily who had a peculiar fascination with mushrooms. She spent every Saturday exploring the forest nearby, convinced that somewhere hidden among the ancient trees, they might discover a magical mushroom.

This year, the festival featured a special exhibit of birdhouses. Lily was thrilled and spent her afternoon building her own tiny birdhouse, hoping it might house a tiny friend.

Later that night, under a sky full of stars, Lily heard a soft tapping outside her window. Curious, she put aside her birdhouse-making. To her delight, a tiny golden-backed woodpecker settled on the windowsill. It drummed a cheerful tune against the side, its eye glowing with curiosity.
